titleInfo.title: A Study of the Dai Temple at Mount Tai

mods.abstract: p. 138: 磚 brick When digging a groove for the installation of a lightning arrester at Renan Gate 仁安門 in January 1985, about 150 cm below ground level, architectural remains came to light. p. 139: Different kinds of 瓦當 eaves-tiles were found, among them four tiles with lotus decor. Diameters vary from 13-15 cm. Beads and lotus leaves. 瓦當 eaves-tiles are also called 筒瓦頭 (hanging edge of a dripping tile) p. 141: In August 1984, eaves-tiles with lotus decor were found at Shentong Monastery 神通寺. Those with eight lotus leavea resemble those unearthed in Daimiao. ( see 王建浩，山東濟南神通寺殿堂遺址的清理。 考古， 1996 年， 第1期。) At the same site in Shentong Si a large-size gutter 大型滴水 was found, p. 142: which is very similar to a gutter 滴水 found in October 2002 in Daimiao, when work was undertaken on the road at the north side of Xihua Gate 西華門. Its decor is from the Tang. Other eaves-tiles found at Ren’an gate date to the Han dyansty, providing evidence that buildings stood there already a long time ago. This must have been the Lower Tempel 下廟 of the three tempels 三廟 already mentioned in the 水經注, with trees from the time of han Wudi. Continuation of construction is given until the High Tang.
